landing.heroTitle: Send money home the smart way
landing.heroSubtitle: SendYurt helps families split between Uzbekistan and abroad compare transfer routes, budget together, and build financial credibility — side by side.
landing.featuresTitle: One home for your family's money

rates.title: Find the best route
rates.body: Compare what your family actually receives across providers — fees and exchange margins included, not just the sticker price.

budget.title: Budget together
budget.body: Senders and receivers see the same picture: spending by category, savings goals, and progress you build as one family.

trust.title: Build financial trust
trust.body: Regular transfers and steady saving become a transparent Trust Score your family can grow month by month.

landing.how1Body: Register as a sender abroad or a receiver at home, and connect through one shared household with a simple invite code.
landing.how2Body: See fees, exchange rates and delivery time side by side, sorted by what actually arrives in soʻm.
landing.how3Body: Track spending, reach savings goals, and watch your family's Trust Score grow with every consistent month.
landing.footerNote: Built with care for families between homes.

assistant.title: Yordam
assistant.subtitle: Your SendYurt helper — ask in Uzbek, Russian or English.
assistant.guideMode: Quick guide mode — answers come from the built-in help.
assistant.welcome: Salom! I'm Yordam. Ask me anything about sending money, the family budget, or your Trust Score — you can also tap the microphone and just speak.
assistant.rateLimited: You've asked a lot in a short time — please wait a little and try again.
assistant.error: I couldn't answer just now. Please try again in a moment.
assistant.guideFallback: I can help with sending money home, the family budget, savings goals, the Trust Score, family members and printable reports. Ask about one of those, or open the Help page for plain-language answers to common questions.

help.title: Help & questions
help.subtitle: Plain answers about what each part of SendYurt does.
help.footer: Still unsure about something? Ask the family member who set up your household, or write to us — SendYurt is built for families, not for banks.

rates.q: What does the Rate Finder do?
rates.a: Before sending money home, it compares several transfer services side by side. Instead of showing only the fee, it calculates what your family actually receives in soʻm after fees and the exchange-rate margin — and sorts by that. You can save your usual amount so the comparison is ready every time you open the page.

budget.q: How does the family budget work?
budget.a: Everyone in your household sees the same budget. You can record expenses and income, set monthly limits for categories like food or education, and create savings goals that the whole family fills together. Charts show where money went and how spending compares with saving month by month.

trust.a: A 0–100 number built from three habits: how regularly money arrives from abroad, how steady the amounts are, and whether the family saves. It is not a credit rating — it's your family's own record, and every factor shows the exact numbers behind it, with tips to improve.

household.q: How do I add family members?
household.a: Open the Family page and share your invite code. A relative enters it when registering and joins your household. Admins can mark members as view-only — useful for younger family members who should see the budget but not change it.

notifications.q: What will I be notified about?
notifications.a: The bell in the top bar shows news that matters: a transfer arriving for your family, a savings goal getting close to complete, or a meaningful change in your Trust Score. Nothing promotional — only your family's money.

report.q: Can I show my history to a bank or lender?
report.a: Yes. The Trust Score page has a shareable report, and the budget page has a financial summary — both are clean documents you can print or save as PDF and show to a microfinance officer. They clearly state what the numbers mean and where they come from.

notifications.title: Notifications

trust.title: Family Trust Score
trust.subtitle: A transparent picture of your family's financial habits — built from regular transfers, steady amounts and saving. Not a credit score: a habit you grow together.
trust.demoNote: this score is calculated from the seeded sample history so you can see how it works.
trust.provisional: provisional — less than 3 months of history
trust.explainer: The score combines three habits over the last 12 months: how regularly money arrives from abroad, how steady the amounts are, and whether the family sets something aside. Every factor below shows the exact numbers behind it.
trust.creditNote: Over time, your Trust Score can serve as a credit history — proof of reliability even without a bank record.

consistency.title: Transfer consistency
consistency.explanation: Money arrived in {active} of the last {total} months. Regular monthly transfers are the strongest signal of reliability.

stability.title: Amount stability
stability.explanation: Monthly transfer amounts varied by about {variation}% around their average. Steadier amounts make the family budget easier to plan.

savings.title: Savings habit
savings.explanation: The family set money aside in {months} of {total} months, saving about {rate}% of what came in. Even small, regular deposits count.

keepGoing.title: Keep building your history
keepGoing.body: The score becomes reliable after 3 months of activity. Keep logging transfers and savings — it will settle quickly.

consistency.title: Send on a rhythm, not just when possible
consistency.body: A transfer every month — even a smaller one — raises your consistency more than occasional large ones. Pick a day of the month and stick to it.

stability.title: Even out the amounts
stability.body: If earnings swing, consider sending a steady base amount monthly and topping up in good months. Predictable amounts help the family plan.

savings.title: Make saving automatic
savings.body: Set a small fixed deposit to a savings goal right after each transfer arrives — before spending starts. Aim for 10% of what comes in.

maintain.title: Keep doing what you're doing
maintain.body: Your habits are strong across the board. Maintain the rhythm and your score will stay high — and your history will show it.

trust.disclaimer: The Trust Score is an internal SendYurt indicator to help families build healthy money habits and document their reliability. It is not a credit rating and is never shared without your consent.
trust.reliabilitySubtitle: Your trust score reflects your commitment to your family's future.
trust.encourage: You're among our most consistent senders this month. Keep it up!
trust.descConsistency: Regular monthly transfers demonstrate financial reliability.
trust.descStability: Predictable transfer amounts reduce risk assessment scores.
trust.descSavings: Consistent saving habits indicate long-term financial health.
trust.howToSubtitle: Improving your score unlocks lower remittance fees and higher daily transfer limits.
trust.actionRecurringDesc: Automate your monthly remittance to boost consistency score instantly.
trust.actionSavingsDesc: Growing your Emergency Fund by 5% this month will lift your stability score.
trust.testimonial: Our top members save an average of 15% more on fees.
trust.creditBody: New to formal banking? Your SendYurt Trust Score doubles as an early credit history — a reliable track record that can help first-time users qualify for small loans or lines of credit from banks.
